questions.prompt: How should a bot detect order fills on Alpaca?

choices.text: Subscribe to the Trading WebSocket (`TradingStream.subscribe_trade_updates`) and receive events in real-time when orders transition through states (new, partial_fill, fill, canceled, rejected). Polling get_order in a loop is wasteful (eats your 200 req/min rate limit), laggy (events lag by your poll interval), and doesn't scale (each open order needs its own poll). WebSocket pushes are real-time, scale to many open orders for free, and put fill data right where you'd react to it. Required pattern for any bot beyond hello-world.

questions.explanation: Subscribe to trade updates via WebSocket — real-time fill events, no polling.

questions.prompt: Your bot has run on paper for 3 days. PnL looks great. Switch to live?

choices.text: No — paper-trade at least 30 days with no manual intervention before going live. Three days isn't enough to surface: rare error paths, weekend behavior, after-hours edge cases, broker outages, market regime shifts. Also verify live execution by funding a tiny live account and running with very small size for another period — paper-fills don't always match live-fills perfectly (no real queue position, simulated price improvement). Required pre-live checklist: 30+ days paper, error paths all handled, kill switch wired and tested, alerts on stop-trading / big-loss conditions, conservative position sizing. Three days of paper is hello-world, not production.

questions.explanation: 30+ days of paper, full error handling, kill switch, alerts, conservative sizing before going live.
